name: xlide
description: Excel VBA integration for VS Code - tree view, edit VBA modules, AI agent tools

xlide.pythonPath.description: Path to the Python executable used to run the XLIDE backend. Leave empty to use 'python' (Windows) or 'python3' (Mac/Linux) from PATH.

xlide.attachToRunningExcel.description: Windows only: when opening a workbook or running a macro, try to attach to a running Excel instance and reuse an already-open workbook before opening it again.

viewsWelcome.contents:
No Excel workbooks (.xlsm, .xlsb, .xlam) were found in the current workspace.
[Refresh](command:xlide.refreshExplorer)

Live Share notes:
- Guests can view and edit any VBA module the host has open in the editor.
- Only the host can browse the XLIDE Explorer and open new modules.
- Guests do not need XLIDE installed to participate.

languageModelTools.name: xlide_listWorkbooks
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Finds all Excel workbooks (.xlsm, .xlsb, .xlam) in the current workspace and returns their absolute paths. Call this first when the user has not specified a file path, or when you need to discover which workbooks are available.

languageModelTools.name: xlide_getWorkbookInfo
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns a combined one-shot summary of an Excel workbook: VBA modules (name + type), worksheets (name + used dimensions), and named ranges. Use this as the first discovery call instead of calling xlide_listModules and xlide_listSheets separately.

languageModelTools.name: xlide_listModules
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists all VBA modules in an Excel .xlsm file. Returns module names and types (standard, class, document). Use this before reading or editing a module.

languageModelTools.name: xlide_listSubs
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists all Sub and Function procedures in a specific VBA module. Returns procedure names, kinds (Sub/Function/Property Get/Let/Set), and line numbers.

languageModelTools.name: xlide_readModule
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reads the full VBA source code of a module from an Excel file. Returns the raw VBA text including Attribute declarations.

moduleName.description: Name of the VBA module to read

languageModelTools.name: xlide_writeModule
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Writes the VBA source code of a module in an Excel file and saves automatically. If the module does not exist it is created (use moduleName to set the name, and optionally pass kind='class' in the source for class modules — default is standard). Use this to apply AI-generated or edited VBA code, or to create new modules from scratch. IMPORTANT: Use only ASCII characters (a-z, A-Z, 0-9, and standard ASCII punctuation) to avoid character mangling. Avoid Unicode, emoji, accented characters, and special symbols.

moduleName.description: Name of the VBA module to write

source.description: The full VBA source code to write into the module

languageModelTools.name: xlide_renameModule
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Renames a VBA module in an Excel workbook and saves the file. Use this to change a module's name. After renaming, any references to the old name in other modules must be updated manually with xlide_writeModule.

moduleName.description: Current name of the VBA module

newName.description: New name for the VBA module

languageModelTools.name: xlide_deleteModule
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Permanently deletes a VBA module from an Excel workbook and saves the file. This cannot be undone. Only delete standard or class modules; avoid deleting document modules (Sheet/ThisWorkbook).

moduleName.description: Name of the VBA module to delete

languageModelTools.name: xlide_listSheets
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists all worksheet names and their used cell ranges (dimensions) in an Excel file. Call this first whenever the sheet name is unknown before using xlide_readCells, xlide_writeCells, or xlide_runOpenpyxl. Returns an array of {name, dimensions} objects where dimensions is in A1 notation (e.g. 'A1:D20') or empty for a blank sheet.

languageModelTools.name: xlide_readCells
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reads computed cell values (not formulas) from a worksheet in an Excel file. Returns a 2D array of values (rows x columns). If the sheet name is unknown, call xlide_listSheets first. To read raw formulas instead, use xlide_readFormulas.

range.description: Cell range in A1 notation, e.g. 'A1:D10'

languageModelTools.name: xlide_readFormulas
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reads raw cell content including formula strings (e.g. '=SUM(A1:A10)') from a worksheet. Use this instead of xlide_readCells when you need to understand or reproduce spreadsheet logic. Returns a 2D array; formula cells contain the formula string, non-formula cells contain their value.

range.description: Cell range in A1 notation, e.g. 'A1:D10'

languageModelTools.name: xlide_writeCells
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Writes values to a range of cells in a worksheet in an Excel file. The file is saved automatically. If the sheet name is unknown, call xlide_listSheets first. IMPORTANT: Use only ASCII characters (a-z, A-Z, 0-9, and standard ASCII punctuation) to avoid character mangling. Avoid Unicode, emoji, accented characters, and special symbols.

startCell.description: Top-left cell in A1 notation, e.g. 'A1'

data.description: 2D array of values to write (rows of columns)

languageModelTools.name: xlide_exportModules
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Exports all VBA modules from a workbook to a folder using workbookname.extension.repo.json behavior shared with UI commands. Uses live module reads from the workbook. Supports exportMode trueUp (default: replace existing, add new, remove stale) and replaceExistingOnly (replace existing files only).

exportFolder.description: Optional absolute folder path. If omitted, uses exportFolder from workbookname.extension.repo.json

exportMode.description: Optional mode override for this run

languageModelTools.name: xlide_configureExportMode
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Sets the persistent exportMode in workbookname.extension.repo.json beside the workbook. Use trueUp by default unless the user explicitly requests replaceExistingOnly.

exportMode.description: Persistent export mode to store in workbookname.extension.repo.json

languageModelTools.name: xlide_runOpenpyxl
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Executes arbitrary Python code with full openpyxl access to an Excel workbook. The code runs in a namespace with: `wb` (open openpyxl Workbook), `openpyxl` (the module), `json` (stdlib json), and `result` (assign your return value here). If `save` is true (default), the workbook is saved after execution. Use this to style cells, add/remove sheets, set column widths, number formats, conditional formatting, charts, named ranges, or any other openpyxl operation. IMPORTANT: Use only ASCII characters in cell values and strings to avoid character mangling. Assign the return value to `result`; captured stdout is also returned.

code.description: Python code to execute. `wb` is the open Workbook, `openpyxl` is the module, `json` is available. Assign your return value to `result`.

save.description: Whether to save the workbook after execution (default: true). Set false for read-only queries.

scripts.vsix: npm run package && node -e "const {execSync}=require('child_process'),{version}=require('./package.json');require('fs').mkdirSync('dist',{recursive:true});execSync('npx vsce package --out dist/xlide-'+version+'.vsix',{stdio:'inherit'})"
